points.text: Immatics N.V. announced four oral presentations at the 2026 ASCO Annual Meeting covering its clinical cell therapy and bispecific portfolio.

Leads Biolabs advanced its PD-L1/4-1BB bispecific antibody Opamtistomig (LBL-024) into the expansion phase of a Phase II trial for first-line biliary tract cancer following a safety run-in of 20 patients showing favorable tolerability.

Medicenna Therapeutics presented preclinical data for MDNA113, a first-in-class anti-PD-1 x IL-2 bifunctional Superkine, at AACR 2026, highlighting its design via BiSKITs™ and T-MASK™ platforms for solid tumors.

Promatix Biosciences presented preclinical data for PBS293-MMAE, a first-in-class EGFR×EphA2 cis-bispecific ADC, demonstrating 50x higher potency than cetuximab-MMAE in KRAS mutant HCT116 colorectal cancer cells.
Enara Bio presented first-in-class DARKFOX-targeting bispecific T cell engager ENA101 at AACR 2026, demonstrating picomolar binding affinity and deep tumor regression in solid tumor xenograft models.
Regeneron Pharmaceuticals utilizes its VelociSuite platform to generate optimized fully human antibodies and new classes of bispecific antibodies for various therapeutic areas.

Immatics N.V. announced four oral presentations at the 2026 ASCO Annual Meeting covering its clinical cell therapy and bispecific portfolio, highlighting PRAME-directed TCR T-cell therapy results in pediatric nephroblastoma.

Leads Biolabs advanced its PD-L1/4-1BB bispecific antibody Opamtistomig (LBL-024) into the expansion phase of a Phase II trial for first-line biliary tract cancer after completing a 20-patient safety run-in with favorable tolerability.
A named-patient experimental treatment using Immatics' PRAME-directed TCR (IMA203CD8) induced deep remission in a 17-year-old with metastatic nephroblastoma, with ongoing response at 6 months.

Opamtistomig (LBL-024) is being evaluated in Phase II trials across three indications including non-small cell lung cancer, small cell lung cancer, and extrapulmonary neuroendocrine carcinoma, with first-line biliary tract cancer now entering expansion.

Medicenna Therapeutics presented preclinical data for MDNA113, a first-in-class anti-PD-1 x IL-2 bifunctional Superkine, at AACR 2026, demonstrating superior safety and efficacy potential for solid tumors.

Promatix Biosciences highlighted its logic-gated proteomics-driven bispecific discovery platform at AACR 2026, presenting preclinical data for PBS293, a first-in-class EGFR×EphA2 cis-bispecific ADC for colorectal cancer.
Promatix's lead bispecific ADC candidate PBS293-MMAE demonstrated 50x greater potency than cetuximab-MMAE in KRAS mutant colorectal cell line HCT116 in preclinical xenograft studies.

Enara Bio presented first-in-class DARKFOX-targeting bispecific T cell engager ENA101 at AACR 2026, demonstrating picomolar binding affinity to the DARKFOX-A3 peptide-HLA complex and deep tumor regression in xenograft models.
No articles explicitly mentioning 'bispecific' antibodies, mechanisms, or direct associations were identified in the provided source material.

Myeloma specialists compared next-generation CELMoDs (iberdomide, mezigdomide) against current standards including bispecific antibodies in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma treatment sequencing discussions.

Expert panels at AACR 2026 and Frontline Forum discussions explicitly positioned bispecific antibodies as a current standard of care for comparison against emerging CELMoDs and CAR-T therapies in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma.

Immatics N.V. — Bispecific Portfolio — Multiple Oncology Indications — Four oral presentations scheduled at 2026 ASCO Annual Meeting highlighting clinical data across bispecific and cell therapy programs.

Leads Biolabs — Opamtistomig (LBL-024) — Biliary Tract Cancer — Phase II expansion phase initiated after 20-patient safety run-in; combination with chemotherapy showed favorable safety profile and preliminary tumor reduction trends.

Medicenna Therapeutics — MDNA113 — Solid Tumors — First-in-class anti-PD-1 x IL-2 bifunctional Superkine; preclinical data presented at AACR 2026 demonstrating superior safety and efficacy potential via BiSKITs™ platform.

Promatix Biosciences — PBS293-MMAE — Colorectal Cancer — First-in-class EGFR×EphA2 cis-bispecific ADC; preclinical data showed 50x potency vs cetuximab-MMAE and superior tumor growth inhibition in HCT116 xenografts via hybrid avidity-gated binding.

Enara Bio — ENA101 — Solid Tumors — First-in-class bispecific T cell engager targeting DARKFOX-A3 peptide-HLA complex; preclinical data shows low pM EC50 values and robust in vivo anti-tumor activity presented at AACR 2026.

Regeneron Pharmaceuticals — VelociSuite platform — Bispecific antibody generation — Proprietary technology produces optimized fully human antibodies and new classes of bispecific antibodies for disease treatment.

Immatics N.V. — PRAME TCR T-cell therapy (IMA203CD8) — Metastatic Nephroblastoma — Named-patient use resulted in deep remission with marked regression of liver, lung, and brain metastases; response ongoing at 6 months; data presented as late-breaking poster at AACR 2026.

Leads Biolabs — Opamtistomig (LBL-024) — First-line Biliary Tract Cancer — Phase II expansion phase initiated following 20-patient safety run-in; combination with chemotherapy showed favorable safety profile and preliminary tumor reduction trends.
Medicenna Therapeutics — MDNA113 — Solid Tumors — Presented positive preclinical data at AACR 2026 for first-in-class anti-PD-1 x IL-2 bifunctional Superkine utilizing BiSKITs™ and T-MASK™ platforms.

Promatix Biosciences — PBS293-MMAE — Colorectal Cancer — Presented preclinical data at 16th World ADC London Summit and AACR 2026 for first-in-class EGFR×EphA2 cis-bispecific ADC showing enhanced tumor selectivity via hybrid avidity-gated binding.

Enara Bio — ENA101 — Solid Tumor Immunotherapy — First-in-class bispecific T cell engager targeting DARKFOX-A3 (HLA-A*03:01) presented at AACR 2026; preclinical data shows picomolar affinity, robust cancer cell killing, and deep tumor regression in xenograft models.

Multiple Myeloma Experts — Bispecific Antibodies — Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma — Referenced as current standard of care in panel discussion comparing efficacy and sequencing against iberdomide (CC-220) and mezigdomide (CC-92480) trials.

Multiple Myeloma Experts — Bispecific Antibodies — Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma — Identified as current standard of care in treatment sequencing discussions, serving as the primary comparator for next-generation CELMoDs (iberdomide, mezigdomide) and CAR-T therapies.

Immatics N.V. — Precision targeting of PRAME via bispecifics and cell therapies — Clinical-stage bispecific portfolio — Presenting data at 2026 ASCO Annual Meeting; company identified as global leader in PRAME-targeted bispecifics.

Leads Biolabs — Bispecific antibody clinical expansion — Opamtistomig (LBL-024) — Phase II expansion in biliary tract cancer; program also active in NSCLC, SCLC, and EP-NEC with first- or best-in-class potential.
Medicenna Therapeutics — Bifunctional Superkine development — MDNA113 (anti-PD-1 x IL-2) — Clinical-stage program for solid tumors utilizing proprietary BiSKITs™ and T-MASK™ platforms.

Promatix Biosciences — Cis-bispecific ADC discovery platform expansion — PBS293-MMAE (EGFR×EphA2) — Utilizing proteomics-driven logic-gated targeting to improve tumor selectivity and reduce toxicity in solid tumors; data presented at 16th World ADC London Summit.

Enara Bio — Dark Antigen discovery and bispecific T cell engager innovation — ENA101 (DARKFOX-targeting) — Utilizing proprietary EnTiCE platform for T cell redirection; advancing into clinical development following positive preclinical xenograft data.

Regeneron Pharmaceuticals — Bispecific antibody development — VelociSuite platform — Proprietary technology enables production of new classes of bispecific antibodies alongside fully human antibodies.

Immatics N.V. — Precision targeting of PRAME via cell therapies and bispecifics — PRAME TCR (IMA203CD8) and bispecific portfolio — Four oral presentations scheduled at 2026 ASCO; named-patient TCR therapy achieved deep remission in pediatric metastatic nephroblastoma.

Leads Biolabs — Development of PD-L1/4-1BB bispecific antibody Opamtistomig — LBL-024 (Opamtistomig) — Phase II expansion in biliary tract cancer; first-line NSCLC, SCLC, and EP-NEC trials ongoing; confirmatory study planned upon data maturation.

Medicenna Therapeutics — Development of bifunctional Superkines for cancer and autoimmune diseases — MDNA113 (anti-PD-1 x IL-2) — Clinical-stage; utilizing proprietary BiSKITs™ and T-MASK™ platforms for solid tumor indications.

Promatix Biosciences — Logic-gated proteomics-driven bispecific ADC discovery — PBS293 (EGFR×EphA2 cis-bispecific ADC) — Preclinical stage; lead program in colorectal cancer demonstrating 50x potency advantage over cetuximab-MMAE in HCT116 cells.

Enara Bio — Dark Antigen discovery and bispecific T cell engager innovation — ENA101 (DARKFOX-targeting) — Utilizing proprietary EnTiCE platform for T cell redirection; advancing lead candidate into clinical development following positive preclinical safety and efficacy data.

Panel Consensus — Treatment Sequencing Strategy — Bispecific Antibodies vs CELMoDs — Identified as established comparator class for iberdomide (CC-220) and mezigdomide (CC-92480) in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma landscape.

Industry Consensus — Treatment Sequencing Strategy — Bispecific Antibodies vs. CELMoDs — Panelists at Frontline Forum (April 2026) highlighted the critical need to refine sequencing strategies between bispecific antibodies and novel agents like iberdomide and mezigdomide.

Immatics Bispecifics — Mechanism: PRAME-targeted bispecific T-cell engagers (implied by portfolio context) — Phase: Clinical — Population: Solid tumors (PRAME-positive) — Endpoints: Data presentations scheduled at 2026 ASCO Annual Meeting.

Opamtistomig (LBL-024) — Mechanism: PD-L1/4-1BB bispecific antibody blocking immune suppression and activating co-stimulatory pathway — Phase: 2 — Population: Advanced biliary tract cancer — Comparator: Chemotherapy combination — Results: Favorable safety in 20-patient run-in; no new safety signals; preliminary tumor reduction observed.

MDNA113 — Mechanism: Anti-PD-1 x IL-2 bifunctional Superkine (BiSKITs™ platform) — Phase: Preclinical — Population: Solid tumors — Comparator: N/A — Results: Positive preclinical data presented at AACR 2026 demonstrating superior safety and efficacy potential.

PBS293-MMAE — Mechanism: Cis-bispecific EGFR×EphA2 hybrid avidity-gated binding — Phase: Preclinical — Population: KRAS mutant colorectal cancer (HCT116) — Comparator: Cetuximab-MMAE — Results: 50x increased potency; enhanced tumor growth inhibition with reduced healthy tissue toxicity.

ENA101 — Mechanism: Bispecific T cell engager (TCE) targeting DARKFOX-A3 peptide-HLA complex via TCR mimic — Phase: Preclinical — Population: DARKFOX-A3+ solid tumor models — Comparator: None stated — Endpoints/Results: Picomolar binding affinity, low pM EC50, deep tumor regression in xenografts with large safety window.

VelociSuite platform — Mechanism: Proprietary antibody engineering — Phase: N/A (Platform description) — Population: N/A — Comparator: N/A — Results: Generates optimized fully human antibodies and new classes of bispecific antibodies.

Opamtistomig (LBL-024) — Mechanism: PD-L1/4-1BB bispecific antibody blocking immune suppression and activating 4-1BB co-stimulation — Phase: 2 — Population: Advanced biliary tract cancer (first-line) — Comparator: Chemotherapy combination — Results: 20-patient safety run-in completed; favorable safety profile, no new signals, encouraging tumor reduction trends.

PRAME-directed TCR (IMA203CD8) — Mechanism: TCR T-cell therapy targeting PRAME antigen — Phase: Experimental (named-patient use) — Population: 17-year-old with advanced metastatic nephroblastoma — Comparator: None (last-resort treatment) — Results: Deep remission at 3 months, ongoing at 6 months; marked regression of liver, lung, and brain metastases.

MDNA113 — Mechanism: Anti-PD-1 x IL-2 bifunctional Superkine (BiSKITs™/T-MASK™ platforms) — Phase: Preclinical — Population: Solid tumors — Results: Demonstrated superior safety and efficacy potential in preclinical models presented at AACR 2026.

PBS293-MMAE — Mechanism: EGFR×EphA2 cis-bispecific ADC with hybrid avidity-gated binding — Phase: Preclinical — Population: KRAS mutant colorectal cancer (HCT116 cell line) — Comparator: Cetuximab-MMAE — Results: 50x higher potency; superior tumor growth inhibition in xenograft studies.

ENA101 — Mechanism: Bispecific T cell engager (TCR mimic) targeting DARKFOX-A3 peptide presented by HLA-A*03:01 — Phase: Preclinical — Population: DARKFOX-A3+ solid tumor models — Comparator: None specified — Results: Picomolar binding affinity, low pM EC50 values, deep tumor regression in xenografts, large preclinical safety window.

Bispecific Antibodies — Mechanism: T-cell redirection (implied by context of comparison) — Phase: Established standard — Population: Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma — Comparator: iberdomide (CC-220) and mezigdomide (CC-92480) — Context: Referenced as current standard for treatment sequencing discussions.

Bispecific Antibodies — Mechanism: T-cell redirecting therapy — Phase: Approved/Standard of Care — Population: Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma — Comparator: Used as benchmark in trials for iberdomide (CC-220-MM-01) and mezigdomide (CC-92480-MM-002) to evaluate next-generation efficacy.
